title: Marbella private yacht hire wedding: costs, tips and boats for 2026
html:
Planning a wedding on the Costa del Sol? Marbella offers a stunning setting for a private yacht ceremony, with departures from Puerto Banús and prices starting around €8,000 for a half-day charter. You can browse a range of skippered charters in Marbella through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat or list your own.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most private yacht weddings depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina known for its superyachts and designer boutiques. Alternatively, you can start from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) for a quieter atmosphere or from La Bajadilla harbour if you prefer a more working-port feel. Popular cruising grounds include the Costa del Sol coastline, the bay of Málaga to the east, and the cliffs of Cabo de la Plata to the south-west. Gibraltar is visible on a clear day, roughly 50 nautical miles south. You can skippered charters in Marbella for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

  1. Skypper motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 63), ideal for an intimate ceremony, sleeps up to 12 guests, from €5,000 half-day. For full specs visit the Sunseeker 63 charter page.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 52), stable and spacious for up to 18 guests, perfect for a cocktail reception, from €6,500 half-day.
  3. Classic motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 55), combines elegance with a flybridge, suitable for 10 guests, from €4,500 half-day.
  4. Luxury superyacht (e.g. Maiora 30m), full crew, jacuzzi, and formal dining for up to 20 guests, from €15,000 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat for a wedding in Marbella?

Prices start at €4,500 for a basic motorboat and exceed €20,000 for a fully crewed superyacht with catering.

  • Shared wedding sunset tour: from €150 per person for a 3-hour cruise around Puerto Banús.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours) private: from €2,500 for up to 8 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours) private: from €8,000 for up to 18 guests, including a skipper and host.
  • Crewed luxury yacht half-day: from €10,000 for up to 20 guests with full catering and drinks.

Do you need a licence to rent a wedding boat in Marbella?

For a private skippered charter you do not need a licence; the operator provides a captain. If you wish to self-drive a small motorboat (e.g. an Axopar 28), a valid ICC or Spanish ''Licencia de Navegación'' is required. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking.

High season in Marbella runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind can bring a calm sea in the morning. Book your wedding charter at least three months ahead for the best choice.

At a glance

  • Price band: €4,500, €20,000+
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day (4h) or full-day (8h)
  • Capacity: 8, 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can we get married on a yacht in Marbella legally?

Yes, you can hold a symbolic ceremony on board, but for a legal wedding you must also perform a civil ceremony ashore or have a captain authorised to conduct marriages (rare in Spain).

What is the best time of day for a wedding charter?

A late afternoon departure around 17:00 lets you exchange vows as the sun sets over the Sierra Blanca, followed by a dinner cruise under the stars.

Is it possible to have a catered reception on a private yacht?

Most crewed yachts offer full catering, including tapas, paella and champagne, arranged by the charter company at an additional cost.

How far in advance should I book a yacht for a summer wedding?

For a wedding in June, July or August, book at least six months ahead; popular models like the Sunseeker 63 are often reserved by February.

Whether you are planning an intimate elopement or a grand celebration, BoatHire24 connects you with trusted boat owners and operators. If you own a yacht in Marbella, you can become a host and earn income from private wedding charters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
